01 — About

Two legacies. One operating system.

I work where AI engineering, product, and capital collide — building systems deep enough to ship and legible enough to fund.

My grandmother, Marguerite Jackson, marched with the NAACP in Alabama during Jim Crow and raised a family alone while working in mental health. My grandfather, Kenji Shintaku, never spoke about the internment camps — I found our family’s names in the Manzanar registry when I was ten. One taught me to seize opportunity out loud; the other taught me that resilience doesn’t always announce itself.

That inheritance turned into a working rule: from everyone who has been given much, much will be demanded. So I ship. Five products from zero to live. Due diligence on 10+ startups with $500K+ deployed behind theses I helped build. Two years of humanitarian work in Spanish in the Dominican Republic. A 17-athlete water polo team captained through multiple tournament wins.

The long game: build products until I understand what founders endure, then back the ones solving hard problems. In the meantime I reset in the ocean — Fountain Valley, California shaped me, and surfing is still where the noise goes quiet.

“You can only change two things in your life: your attitude and your effort.”

The filter for every decision
02 — Experience

The ledger so far.

Engineering, growth, venture, and banking — each row is a seat I’ve actually sat in, with the numbers that came out of it.

  1. Mar 2026 — PresentSan Clemente, CA

    Full-Stack Software Engineer

    Tech Force Advisors
    • Built a retrieval-augmented (RAG) analytics system, deployed internally and client-facing, that surfaces key insights from unstructured firm data.
    • Developed a live content-generation tool in React for B2B outreach — lifted marketing productivity 80% and drove a 5× increase in customer acquisition across social channels.
    5×customer acquisition
  2. Jan 2026 — PresentProvo, UT

    Founder & Head Engineer

    CareBridge Connect
    • Built a full-stack, HIPAA-compliant healthcare platform that surfaces patient well-being indicators for families and skilled nursing facilities.
    • Created an automated CRM covering 200+ Western U.S. healthcare facilities, producing 50+ warm leads for customer discovery.
    • Raised $8,000+ in grants to fund initial development and outreach.
    $8K+grants raised

    Growth Lead

    Perplexity AI
    • Owned campus growth strategy, leading 20+ events that established BYU as Perplexity’s top-performing university for AI-tooling adoption.
    • Designed an agentic-browser registration workflow that automated event sign-ups and eliminated manual coordination.
    • Convened students, faculty, and staff around responsible AI — ethics, labor impact, and policy.
    20+campus events led

    Venture Capital Investment Analyst

    Nucleus Fund
    • Conducted due diligence on 10+ early-stage startups through market research, expert calls, and financial analysis.
    • Built investment theses and pitched opportunities that informed $500K+ in equity deployments across portfolio companies.
    • Partnered with founders and analysts to quantify growth impact and deliver decision-ready analyses and pitch decks.
    $500K+deployments informed

    Commercial Banking Summer Analyst

    Genesis Bank
    • Supported the acquisition of a publicly traded bank — financial due diligence and Phase 1–2 request-list refinement in CapIQ and FactSet.
    • Partnered with the President/COO to plan and execute a large-scale loan backfile, beating the one-time cost savings modeled in the deal.
    • Delivered a market-expansion proposal to senior leadership identifying $30M+ in potential loan and deposit growth.
    $30M+growth identified
03 — Projects

Built, shipped, measured.

Not mockups. Products with users, compliance constraints, and revenue models — each one a bet on where AI actually replaces a workflow.

P.01 — Healthcare · B2B SaaS

CareBridge Connect

A HIPAA-compliant platform that eliminates 2–3 hours of nurse call burden per shift by translating clinical documentation into plain-language family updates. Pivoted from consumer to B2B after 15+ discovery interviews surfaced who actually holds the budget: facility operators.

  • Event-based care timeline surfaced from existing facility documentation
  • Guardrailed AI chat for 24/7 family inquiries — constrained to verified care-record data
  • Auditable retrieval layer between every query and the LLM
Live Next.jsTypeScriptSupabaseOpenAI APIHIPAA

Case study· carebridgeconnect.ai

CareBridge · Daily update AI · guardrailed

“Mom had a great week — her appetite improved and she attended three group activities. No changes to her medication.”

Nurse call burden−2–3 hrs/shift
Care events surfacedautomatic
Family questions24/7 AI chat
Verified against care recordaudit-logged
P.02 — Professional services · Automation

Compass

Business development automation for professional services firms. Monitors commercial triggers — court filings, funding rounds, executive hires — scores prospects with a proprietary relevance algorithm, and drafts contextual outreach for human review. Rainmaking as a system, not a habit.

  • Signal Library: modular monitoring of real-world commercial events
  • R-Score: recency 50% · industry match 30% · decision-maker authority 20%
  • Daily digest with approve / edit / reject — human stays in the loop
In development FastAPIReactPostgreSQLOpenAI APIDocker

Case study

Compass · Daily digest 3 signals
Series B — Meridian HealthR·92
Court filing — Delaware Ch.R·87
CFO hire — Lattice LegalR·74

Hook drafted: “Saw Tuesday’s Series B announcement — congratulations. Most teams revisit their data-processing agreements at this stage…”

Human-in-the-loop reviewapprove · edit · reject
P.03 — Consumer · Marketplace

Eternally

A digital memorial platform — 10,000+ memorials created — that turns cemeteries into interactive living memorials. Consumer product on the front (collaborative storytelling, AI photo restoration), marketplace on the back (remote grave services cemetery operators can white-label).

  • Life-history timeline for collaborative family storytelling
  • AI-enhanced photo restoration via Cloudinary pipelines
  • Remote services marketplace: flowers, cleaning, and decorations placed by local providers
Live Next.jsReactCloudinaryVercel

Eternally · Memorial family-only

A life remembered, 1931–2018 — forty-seven timeline entries, contributed by three generations.

P.04 — Open source · Genealogy

My Story — FamilySearch Agent

An open-source agent harness for the FamilySearch database that helps people find and connect ancestors — then writes a living family log describing the period, culture, and economics each ancestor lived through.

Agent harnessFamilySearch APIOpen source
GitHub
P.05 — Open source · Markets

Economic Indicator Analysis

An open-source economic indicator system spanning buy- and sell-side markets, integrating proprietary and public data sources — with an AI tooling layer that automates research compilation, reporting, and distribution.

PythonMarket dataAI reporting
GitHub

05 — Education & toolkit

Trained on both sides of the table.

The degree is strategy plus statistics. The practice is shipping production systems and reading financial statements — the same problem from two seats.

06 — Writing

Working notes, not takes.

Essays from someone who ships products and sits in diligence calls — the overlap is where the interesting problems live.

  • Mar 2026

    Agentic Systems Are the Next Infrastructure Layer

    We’re moving from AI as a tool to AI as an actor. The architecture implications are enormous.

     →
  • Feb 2026

    What I Learned Building HIPAA-Compliant AI

    Most “secure AI” products skip the hard parts. What actually matters when you’re handling health data.

     →
  • Jan 2026

    Why Most AI Integrations Fail

    Companies are bolting AI onto existing workflows. The ones that win are rebuilding the workflow around AI.

     →
07 — Open problems

What I’d build next.

Gaps I’ve hit firsthand — as an analyst, a builder, and a student moving between finance and technology. Observed in practice, not in theory.

/01

Institutional-grade research for retail investors

+

At Nucleus Fund I used CapIQ, FactSet, and PitchBook daily. Retail investors make allocation decisions with a fraction of that information — and the tools they do have optimize for engagement, not rigor.

The opportunity: an AI research platform that synthesizes SEC filings, earnings transcripts, and sentiment into structured briefs. The moat is editorial quality and source verification, not aggregation.

/02

Deal flow screening that actually uses ML

+

I’ve screened hundreds of pitch decks. The initial filter is pattern matching — thesis fit, team credibility, timing — which is exactly what ML does well. Most firms still run deal flow through shared inboxes and spreadsheets.

The opportunity: tools that pre-screen against a firm’s thesis and surface founder signals before competitors see them. The winners own the data layer, not the UI.

/03

Financial literacy that changes behavior

+

Smart, driven peers headed to Goldman and McKinsey don’t understand tax-advantaged accounts or equity compensation. The consequences compound for decades.

The opportunity: financial literacy isn’t knowledge transfer — it’s behavior change, which requires simulations and feedback loops, not articles.

/04

A unified data layer for small business lending

+

At Genesis Bank I watched loan officers spend days chasing clean financials from applicants — not because the data didn’t exist, but because it was trapped in five tools that don’t talk to each other. That directly raises the borrower’s cost of capital.

The opportunity: integration layers that produce bank-ready financials automatically. Lending preparation is the wedge; the borrower–lender relationship is the prize.

/05

Alternatives access for the other 95%

+

The best risk-adjusted returns of the last two decades came from PE, VC, and real assets — and most Americans can’t touch them. Regulation is evolving faster than the infrastructure.

The opportunity: the hard part isn’t the UI — it’s legal architecture, education, and compliance. Whoever solves the full stack builds a durable franchise.

Building something hard?

AI engineering, product, venture — if the problem sits where those meet, I want to hear about it. I read everything sent to my inbox.
